text: You're looking at the view through the surgical microscope.
a small bare hole was placed adjacent to the sinus and then one inch bone flap was elevated using the side cutting drill Duro was exposed
and DACOP sutures were placed at the edge of the dura.

sodium fluorescein microscopy was used to inspect the parenchyma of the exposed parietal lobe arachnoid knife was used to dissect out a large draining vein in order to preserve it during tumor resection
This vein is anterior parietal vein and it drains to superior sagittal sinus.
Using micro bipolar cautery as well as micro suction, transcortical approach was used to expose the superficial pole of the tumor.
After the tumor was identified, a border between the tumor and the brain parenchyma was carefully established using bipolar cautery and microsuction.
The tumor was removed in one piece and was sent for pathological evaluation.
Duro was re-approximated, subsequent to which bone flap was also re-approximated and skin was closed.
